commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=42ab7c5722a08a6cdc28aca34e23b49b813a51ca)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Telegram lane-delivery state to suppress later terminal final-error replies after one terminal error has been delivered, plus a mocked dispatch regression test.

PR surface: Source +35, Tests +25. Total +60 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main lacks terminal-error delivery state, and the PR's mocked test models duplicate final-error payloads; I did not run a live Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are a concrete progress-mode code defect plus contributor-side Telegram proof, so this should stay in human review rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(telegram): dedupe terminal error replies
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Telegram users could receive two visible terminal error replies for one failed request when the runner reported duplicate final failure payloads, for example a specific timeout message followed by a generic fallback error.

## Why This Change Was Made

This change records terminal error delivery in Telegram lane-delivery state and suppresses later duplicate terminal final-error replies after one terminal error has already been delivered. Non-terminal tool warning payloads remain outside this suppression path so tool warnings can still be surfaced when appropriate.

The change is intentionally scoped to Telegram delivery behavior. It does not alter model execution, retry behavior, or non-Telegram channel delivery.

AI-assisted: yes; the final patch was reviewed with focused local tests and type/lint checks.

## User Impact

Telegram users should see one clear terminal failure message instead of a timeout/error pair for the same failed request. This reduces noisy duplicate error replies while preserving the first meaningful failure message.

## Evidence

Local verification on commit `2135ebd3f44b1d1a00b999e15e12a7613845a75c`:

- `git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <3 changed files>` passed.
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` passed: 135 tests.
- Targeted oxlint on the changed Telegram files passed with 0 warnings / 0 errors.
- Extension test typecheck passed:
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- Extension prod typecheck passed:
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --noEmit`

Native Telegram behavior proof is still required by repository policy; requesting a Mantis Telegram Desktop proof run in PR comments.
target.author: martysai

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
